serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml aluminium can of Coca-Cola, as commonly served in Singapore and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for regular cola carbonated beverage with caffeine. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml). Coca-Cola standard formulation is consistent with this reference.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, carbonated soft drink — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney
gi_source.source_match_notes: Coca-Cola regular has a published GI of approximately 63 (medium) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving calculated as: (63 × 35.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 22.1. Available carbohydrate per 330 ml serving = 10.6 g/100 ml × 3.30 = 35.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (4 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola contains negligible sodium (approximately 13 mg per 330 ml can), so it does not directly raise blood pressure through sodium load; however, its high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic dysfunction that indirectly elevates blood pressure with habitual consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content and medium glycemic index (GI 63)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 330 ml can delivers approximately 35 g of free sugars with a glycemic load of 22, causing a rapid postprandial glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption and substitute with sugar-free or zero-calorie alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia and visceral adiposity
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Coca-Cola is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased visceral fat, all of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola (Canned) is a globally recognised carbonated cola soft drink sold in a standard 330 ml aluminium can. It is manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company and is one of the most widely consumed beverages in the world, including in Singapore where it is commonly served at Chinese restaurants, hawker centres, and through food delivery platforms. The beverage is ultra-processed, containing carbonated water, sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. It has a characteristic sweet, slightly acidic taste with a strong effervescence and a dark brown colour. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 41 kcal, 10.6 g of carbohydrate (entirely as free sugars), negligible protein, and negligible fat. A standard 330 ml can therefore delivers approximately 135 kcal and 35 g of free sugars. The glycemic index is approximately 63 (medium), and the glycemic load per can is approximately 22, making it a significant glycemic challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. It contains no dietary fibre, no meaningful vitamins, and only trace minerals. From a clinical nutrition perspective, regular consumption is associated with increased risk of obesity, Type 2 Diabetes, dental caries, dyslipidaemia, and cardiovascular disease. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is gluten-free and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
How much sugar is in a Coca-Cola can?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of Coca-Cola?
How much caffeine in a can of Coke?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851) for regular cola carbonated beverage, which is an exact match to Coca-Cola standard formulation. GI value sourced from University of Sydney GI Database with a published GI of 63 for Coca-Cola.
notes: Protein_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — contains no protein by product formulation. Fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — contains no fat by product formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a sugar-water carbonated beverage with no plant fibre. Vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — Coca-Cola is not fortified and contains no meaningful vitamins. The food is listed under Chinese cuisine in the input due to its restaurant context (Wan Jia Fu Chinese Restaurant), but its region of origin is the United States; multilingual naming follows Singaporean dish rules given the Singapore restaurant context. Serving size is 330 ml (one standard can), with density approximated at 1.0 g/ml for nutritional calculation purposes. Price data from field survey data (SGD 3.62) is preserved as contextual metadata but not included in the nutritional record.
